architecture.summary: The system fine-tunes two off-the-shelf LLMs (GPT-4 and Mistral 7B) on curated question–answer datasets derived from ontology engineering textbooks, converted to JSONL format. No novel model architecture or inference mechanism is introduced; fine-tuning follows standard supervised learning on domain-specific QA pairs extracted from foundational OE texts and visual descriptions translated to description logic.

biblio.title: Fine-Tuning Large Language Models for Ontology Engineering: A Comparative Analysis of GPT-4 and Mistral

claims.dataset: ontology engineering QA dataset from foundational textbooks
claims.statement: GPT-4 demonstrated superior accuracy and adherence to ontology syntax, albeit with higher computational costs

claims.dataset: ontology engineering QA dataset from foundational textbooks
claims.statement: Mistral 7B excelled in speed and cost efficiency but struggled with domain-specific tasks, often generating outputs that lacked syntactical precision and relevance
Fine-tuned LLMs would outperform base models
Domain-specific datasets would significantly improve their performance
Both models, despite their potential in understanding OE principles, exhibited limitations underscoring the importance of aligning training data with domain-specific knowledge
